An NWS Cleveland Storm Survey determined that an EF-0 tornado occurred north of Elmore on June 18, 2025. The tornado was brief, touching down on the northwest corner of Ohio State Route 163 and Elliston Trowbridge Road. The tornado damaged a metal outbuilding and destroyed a grain silo. The tornado crossed Elliston Trowbridge Road and damaged the roof of a residence and snapping a couple trees. Debris was lofted into a field beyond the residence before lifting before Graytown Road. The damage was consistent with an EF-0 tornado with an estimated maximum wind speed of 80 mph.

An NWS Cleveland Storm Survey determined that an EF-0 tornado occurred northeast of Rocky Ridge on June 18, 2025. The tornado touched down on the west side of Toussaint Portage Road and moved east, producing some minor tree damage, including one tree onto a house. Another home on Toussaint Portage Road sustained minor roof and facade damage and had a window shattered. The tornado continued northeast to Genzman Road and brought down more tree branches. The tornado lost intensity as it moved further east. However, a roof of a small barn on Fick Road was partially torn off before finally lifting. The damage was consistent with an EF-0 tornado with an estimated maximum wind speed of 70 mph.

An NWS Cleveland Storm Survey determined that an EF-1 tornado occurred from near Collins in Huron County to northwest of Kipton in Lorain County. The tornado began near the intersection of US Route 20 and Hartland Center Road south of Collins. The tornado mainly produced tree damage along its path with numerous hardwood and softwood trees snapped. Some trees and limbs fell onto area residences causing minor damage. The tornado crossed Derussey Road, Wakeman Townline Road, and Fitchville River Road north of US 20 before approaching Wakeman. The main circulation bypassed the main Wakeman village area with larger trees downed on River Road and Ohio State Route 60 north of the village. However, strong rear flank downdraft straight-line winds moved through Wakeman, allowing for some minor and less concentrated tree damage across the village. The tornado continued northeast toward the Huron-Lorain County line, entering Lorain County near the Firelands Boy Scout Reservation. The tornado continued across Gore Orphanage and Becker Roads, where there was minor roof damage to a home and more downed trees noted, before dissipating. The straight-line winds south of the main circulation were increasing at this time and caused failure of a roof of a large barn and several large uprooted trees on Bates Road. These winds continued across Henrietta Township. The damage was consistent with an EF-1 tornado with an estimated maximum wind speed of 100 mph.

An NWS Cleveland storm survey determined that an EF-0 tornado occurred from Chatham Township to Lafayette Township in Medina County OH. The tornado began near the intersection of Spencer Lake Road and Ballou Road. The tornado mainly produced tree damage along its path with multiple trees snapped. The tornado moved southeast across Spencer Lake Road and damaged an unoccupied small barn near the intersection of Spencer Lake Road and Erhart Road. The tornado continued southeast towards Carsten Road where there was minor roof damage to a home and several large tree branches snapped. The damage was consistent with an EF-0 tornado with an estimated maximum wind speed of 81 mph.
